title: Say Goodbye to Upset Stomach with These 8 Best Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort.
Shogaol is good for upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, and also relaxes the muscles in the stomach to ease nausea and cramps.
Gingerols are good for upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is good for an upset stomach because of its natural ingredients. Ginger has special powers that help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. When you eat something that doesn't agree with you, it can make your stomach feel sick and uncomfortable. The ginger in the cream works by relaxing the muscles in your stomach and reducing inflammation. This helps to soothe and calm your stomach, making you feel better. Ginger cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can help reduce the pain and discomfort in your stomach. Additionally, ginger has natural digestive enzymes that help break down food and ease digestion. When you apply the ginger cream to your stomach or have it orally, the active ingredients are absorbed quickly into your system, providing fast relief from an upset stomach.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with an upset stomach, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, gather aloe vera gel from a mature aloe plant, and extract its thick, transparent juice with a spoon or a specialized tool. Next, combine 1/2 cup of the aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. As the mixture melts, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil for its soothing properties and a teaspoon of ginger powder for its anti-inflammatory effects. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, transfer it to a clean container and refrigerate it for a few hours to solidify. After it has set, your Aloe vera herbal cream is ready to use. Simply apply a small amount to the affected area, and let it absorb into your skin to help calm and soothe your stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin in Aloe Vera is good for upset stomach because it helps to soothe and calm the digestive system by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach.
Flavonoids are good for upset stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Saponins are good for an upset stomach because they help calm down the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and soothe the lining of the intestines, making them easier to digest.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy for upset stomach. It contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. When you have an upset stomach, it can become irritated and inflamed, leading to pain and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin in the cream works by blocking the production of a chemical called cortisol, which can make the stomach muscles contract and cause more pain. This helps to relax the stomach muscles and reduce the discomfort. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and soothe the stomach lining. By reducing inflammation and calming the stomach muscles, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help to provide quick relief from an upset stomach and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with an upset stomach, first you need to make an Aloe vera gel. Cut an Aloe vera leaf from a mature plant and slice it open to remove the yellow sap. Rinse the clear gel with water and then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining yellow sap. Next, mix the Aloe vera gel with a carrier oil like coconut oil or olive oil in a ratio of 1:1. For example, you can mix 1 cup of Aloe vera gel with 1 cup of coconut oil. Then, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil or ginger essential oil, which are known for their soothing effects on the stomach. Finally, stir well and pour the mixture into a glass jar. This cream can be applied topically to the abdomen to help soothe an upset stomach. Note: Before using any new herbal remedy, it's always best to do a patch test and consult with a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for upset stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for upset stomach because it helps to calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation and relieve nausea by numbing the stomach area and allowing it to heal faster.
Rosmarinic acid is good for an upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, allowing the stomach to heal and relax.
Menthone in Aloe Vera is good for an upset stomach because it helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and ease cramps, which can help to calm nausea and other stomach discomforts.
Luteolin is good for upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and soothe the digestive tract, helping to calm nausea and alleviate digestive discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy for an upset stomach. When you eat something that doesn't agree with you, it can cause discomfort, nausea, and stomach pain. Peppermint cream can help calm your stomach and reduce these symptoms. The menthol in peppermint works by relaxing the muscles in your stomach, allowing it to move food more easily and reducing cramps. It also helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can become irritated when you have an upset stomach. Additionally, peppermint has natural antispasmodic properties, which can help to stop stomach spasms that can cause pain and discomfort. When you apply peppermint cream to your stomach, the menthol is absorbed quickly, providing fast relief from stomach upset.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream for an upset stomach, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you need to extract the gel from an Aloe vera leaf - cut a leaf from a mature plant, peel off the outer skin, and scoop out the clear gel inside. Next, mix the Aloe vera gel with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:1 ratio. You will also need to add a few drops of peppermint essential oil for its soothing properties, and a few drops of ginger essential oil to help ease nausea. Now, add a tablespoon of beeswax to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly, then whip it with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream. Finally, pour the cream into a container and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. To use, apply a small amount to your stomach and massage it gently before bed or whenever you need relief from an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for upset stomach

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural remedy for an upset stomach. The herb contains a chemical called anethole, which helps to soothe the digestive system. When you eat something that doesn't agree with you, your stomach can become inflamed and irritated. Fennel cream can calm this inflammation and reduce the discomfort. It also helps to break down gas in the digestive system, which can contribute to bloating and cramping. Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties can help to protect the lining of the stomach and reduce the risk of ulcers. Furthermore, fennel cream can help to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation or diarrhea. This can help to reduce the symptoms of an upset stomach, such as nausea and stomach pain. Fennel's natural properties make it a popular choice for people who suffer from digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal creams that help with upset stomach, you need a few simple ingredients. First, you need pure Aloe vera gel, which can be extracted from the Aloe vera plant or bought from a store. You also need coconut oil, which helps to soothe the stomach, and essential oils like peppermint or ginger, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to calm the stomach. To make the cream, you need to mix the Aloe vera gel with coconut oil in a bowl until well combined. Then, add a few drops of the essential oil and mix well again. Next, transfer the mixture to a container and refrigerate it for about an hour to let it cool and set. Once set, your Aloe vera herbal cream is ready to use. To apply it, simply take a small amount and rub it onto your stomach area, where it will help to soothe and calm any upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams can help with an upset stomach because of the alkaloids it contains, like quinine and quinidine. These compounds have been used for a long time to treat various health issues, including digestive problems. Quinine, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines. This can be beneficial for people with stomach ulcers or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The cream may also help soothe the stomach lining, making it easier for food to digest and reducing nausea and vomiting. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach from damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream to help with an upset stomach, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, you need to extract the gel from an Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the edges and the top, then scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, you need to mix 1/2 cup of the Aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts. Then, add 2 tablespoons of calendula oil and 2 tablespoons of chamomile oil, which are known for their soothing properties on the stomach. Continue heating the mixture until it reaches 120-130°F, then remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly. Once it's cooled enough to pour into a container, let it cool completely and solidify. Once it's set, you can apply a small amount of the cream to your stomach to help soothe any discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for an upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach. The cream also has soothing and calming properties, which can help to ease irritation and discomfort in the stomach. When you have an upset stomach, it can be inflamed and irritated, causing pain and discomfort. Aloe barbadensis cream can help to calm this inflammation and irritation, making you feel better. It also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off any infections that may be causing your upset stomach. This can help your stomach to heal faster and feel better sooner. Aloe barbadensis cream is also gentle on the stomach, so it won't make your symptoms worse. This makes it a good option for people who have an upset stomach and are looking for a natural remedy to help them feel better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make aloe vera herbal cream for an upset stomach, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: aloe vera gel, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and essential oils like peppermint or ginger. First, extract the aloe vera gel from a fresh aloe vera leaf, making sure to only take the clear gel and avoid the yellow latex. Next, in a double boiler, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax together until they form a smooth mixture. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before adding the aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and essential oils. Mix everything well until it's fully incorporated and the cream has cooled to room temperature. Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before using. Once it's set, apply a small amount to the affected area or stomach to help soothe and calm the discomfort, and repeat as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus cream, also known as boldo cream, is a traditional remedy for upset stomach. It contains a mixture of the herb boldo, which has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. The cream works by reducing inflammation and calming the stomach muscles, making it easier to digest food. Boldo contains compounds that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. When applied topically, the cream helps to ease stomach pain and discomfort, making it a popular choice for people who experience heartburn, indigestion, and stomach cramps after eating. The cream is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the growth of bacteria that can contribute to stomach problems. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the stomach muscles, Peumus boldus cream can help to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with an upset stomach, you will need a few simple ingredients. First, you need to extract the gel from an Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the green outer skin and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, mix 1/4 cup of the Aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a bowl. You can also add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. In a separate bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of water with 1 tablespoon of cornstarch to create a thickening agent. Gradually add the cornstarch mixture to the Aloe vera mixture, stirring constantly until it thickens into a smooth cream. Pour the cream into a small container, let it cool, and then apply it to the stomach area to help soothe and calm the digestive system. Note that this cream is for external use only and is not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult a doctor if your stomach issues persist.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with an upset stomach, you will need Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and peppermint essential oil. First, extract the Aloe vera gel from a fresh Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the thick green skin and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt the coconut oil and shea butter together until they become smooth and liquid. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly, then stir in the Aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil. After that, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil, which has a soothing effect on the stomach, to the mixture and stir well. Finally, pour the mixture into a small bowl or container and let it cool completely before transferring it to a jar or container. This Aloe vera herbal cream can be applied topically to the stomach area to help soothe and calm an upset stomach.

intro_desc: Using herbal creams can help ease an upset stomach. Two herbs commonly used for this purpose are Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root. Ginger is a natural anti-inflammatory that can help reduce nausea and vomiting. It works by blocking the chemicals in the brain that trigger these symptoms, allowing the stomach to relax and recover. Licorice root, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. It also has a mild laxative effect, which can help relieve constipation and promote digestive health.  An upset stomach can cause more than just discomfort - it can also lead to fatigue, irritability, and decreased productivity. When the stomach is not functioning properly, the body may not be able to absorb essential nutrients, leading to a range of symptoms from mild to severe. Fixing the issue of an upset stomach can improve your quality of life in many ways. By using herbal creams containing ginger and licorice root, you can reduce nausea and vomiting, alleviate digestive discomfort, and promote overall digestive health. This can lead to increased energy levels, improved mood, and a greater sense of well-being. By addressing the root cause of the issue, you can take control of your health and make positive changes that can have a lasting impact on your life.
supplementary_best: A combination of Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) herbal creams can help remedy an upset stomach. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea. Licorice root, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help heal the stomach lining and prevent bacterial overgrowth.  The combination of these two herbs works well because ginger's warming properties can help stimulate digestion, while licorice root's cooling properties can help calm the stomach. This balance of warming and cooling effects can help restore the stomach's natural balance and reduce discomfort. When applied topically as a cream, these properties can be absorbed through the skin and into the bloodstream to help alleviate stomach issues.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an upset stomach, there are some herbal creams you should avoid using. One of these is those that contain 'Echinacea', a plant often used to boost the immune system. However, its high concentration of compounds that stimulate the stomach and intestines can make digestion worse, especially for people with existing stomach problems. Another is 'Ginkgo biloba', a herb known for improving blood flow and memory. However, it can irritate the stomach and make digestive issues like acid reflux worse. Also, avoid 'Cassia auriculata' or 'Senna', as they are known to stimulate the bowels and can worsen stomach upset, diarrhea, and other digestive issues. 'Silybum marianum' or 'St Mary's Thistle', while often used to support liver health, can be too harsh for the stomach and cause more irritation if not used carefully. Lastly, 'Valeriana officinalis' or 'Valerian root' may cause stomach upset due to its ability to slow digestion, making it harder for the body to absorb nutrients. If you're unsure about any herbal creams or their ingredients, it's best to consult a healthcare professional for advice.

When consumed, aloe vera juice can help calm the stomach lining and reduce irritation, making it easier to digest food. It can also help stimulate digestion and relieve bloating. When consumed in the form of tea or added to food, ginger can help calm the stomach and promote a sense of well-being.  Activated charcoal is a natural substance that can help absorb toxins and gas in the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort. When taken as a supplement, activated charcoal can help neutralize stomach acid and soothe the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food. When consumed in the form of tea or added to food, slippery elm can help protect the stomach lining from acid and reduce irritation, making it easier to digest food. This can be especially helpful for people with stomach ulcers or those who experience stomach cramps and diarrhea.  Probiotics are live bacteria and yeast that can help promote a healthy gut microbiome. When taken as a supplement, probiotics can help restore the balance of good bacteria in the stomach, reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. This can be especially helpful for people with IBS or those who experience stomach cramps and diarrhea.  Bentonite clay is a natural substance that can help absorb toxins and gas in the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort. When taken as a supplement, bentonite clay can help neutralize stomach acid and soothe the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food.

These symptoms can be caused by an imbalance of gut bacteria, inflammation, or food sensitivities. Medicinal herbs like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can help alleviate these symptoms.  Peppermint oil can relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can ease nausea and abdominal pain. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate diarrhea. Chamomile tea can calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation, making it easier to digest food.  People with upset stomachs may also experience symptoms like bloating, gas, and indigestion.
